notes: Self-correction iteration for Gemini CLI: within ONE user turn the agent calls run_shell_command `false` (fails), inspects the failing functionResponse, retries with run_shell_command `echo recovered`, then replies 'done'. The recording proves the whole arc is a SINGLE contiguous working span on the transcript-backed session — no flicker to ready or waiting between the failed attempt and the recovery. WHY no flicker (parser.go + tailer.go): the failing tool output is a user-role functionResponse routed to function_call_output WITHOUT ClearToolNames so it never settles (parser.go:133-140); the error/cancelled tool status sets IsError only, which the classifier explicitly ignores (tailer.go:991-992); the recovery assistant message carries toolCalls so it stays assistant_message, NOT turn_done (parser.go:209-211). State settles to ready only on the final text-only 'done' message (non-empty content, zero toolCalls → turn_done). The driver runs `gemini -y` so neither shell call trips a permission gate → never waiting. ANCHORING (reconciled to the live recording 2026-06-11-23-08-19): the daemon mints a presession `proc-<pid>` born ready BEFORE the transcript-backed `session-<ts>` is discovered (pid_bind→start pattern). session_birth matches that presession ready; the turn lives entirely on the real session, so turn_start matches the real session's `working` (no same_session_as on session_birth — that proc never goes working), and intermediate_working / turn_end pin to turn_start's session to assert the whole arc is one session. intermediate_working pins the no-flicker invariant with duration_at_least_ms 2000 — long enough to span both retry cycles given the ~25s working span; if the parser/classifier ever surfaced a failing tool_result (or an intermediate text-only message between the two shell calls) as a transition to ready or waiting, this phase fires. PROVISIONAL→SETTLED: the live recording confirmed Gemini emits no intermediate text-only message between the failing and recovery shell calls (the retry rides entirely on tool_use→functionResponse cycles), so the no-flicker invariant holds. observations assert the verify vector against the recording: model=gemini-3.5-flash (the exact model id the run reported), non-zero cost and tokens, agent=gemini-cli.

text: Session appears in ready shortly after gemini launch (matches the presession proc-<pid> born ready in the pid_bind→start pattern)

User prompt drives the transcript-backed session ready → working; the failing run_shell_command `false` and its corrective `echo recovered` retry both run inside this span (spec bullet 1). No same_session_as on session_birth: the turn fires on the real session, not the presession proc.

State stays continuously working across all retry cycles — the failing `false` functionResponse must NOT flicker the session to ready or waiting before the `echo recovered` recovery cycle starts (spec bullet 2)
After the recovery succeeds the agent replies 'done' (final text, zero tool calls) → working → ready; state never entered waiting (spec bullet 3)
